todays_post.title: Thursday March 12 | Sales | SLAY | Familiar | CTA: B
todays_post.content:
# Thursday March 12 | Sales | SLAY | Familiar | CTA: B

![Post Image](2026-03-12-dad-story.png)

---

My dad never understood what I do.
Last week he finally did.

He's 72. Retired engineer. Practical man.

For 20 years, every time someone asked him what his son does, he'd say: "Something with computers."

He wasn't wrong. He just couldn't picture it.

Last week, he came to visit.

I showed him one screen.
Not a presentation. Not a diagram.

A live dashboard showing patient flow across 15 hospitals in 3 countries.

Real-time bed occupancy. Clinical alerts. Pending discharges. Revenue per episode.

All feeding from systems that didn't talk to each other 18 months ago.

He stared at it for a full minute.

Then he said: "So you built the nervous system."

That's it.
That's the job.

I've spent 20 years building nervous systems for organizations.

At Intel, it was the network infrastructure connecting LTE towers.
At PaySky, it was Egypt's first SuperApp connecting payments, loans, and insurance in one platform.
At Talabat, it was the operations layer that scaled from 30,000 daily orders to 7 million.
At Network International, it was the PMO connecting 300+ banking projects across 8 countries.

And now: a $50M transformation connecting 15 hospitals across 3 countries.

Every one of those jobs had a different title.
None of them had a simple answer to "what do you do?"

But my dad got it in 30 seconds.

Because the job was always the same.

**Find the broken connections. Build the ones that matter.**

The title changes. The work doesn't.

What's the clearest someone has ever described complex work back to you?

..

By the way, I've been documenting my journey managing a $50M hospital transformation across 3 countries. If you're navigating a similar challenge, happy to share what's working. Drop a comment or DM.

#Leadership #DigitalTransformation #HealthTech #Career #GCC

posts.title: TG Mason's Post
posts.author: Tg Mason B8A6421B
posts.topic: Our team is proud to be a part of the Kingdoms transformation into a world class destination ... KSIA, we're not just building an airport — we're
posts.ready_comment: Mega-infrastructure projects like KSIA reveal something most people overlook: the technology backbone is what separates a world-class destination from just a world-class building. Having led a $50M digital transformation across 15 hospitals under Saudi Vision 2030, I've seen firsthand how ambitious national mandates live or die by the integration decisions made early in the program. The complexity of coordinating that many stakeholders, systems, and timelines in a regulated environment teaches you things no blueprint can prepare you for. What's been your biggest lesson in keeping the human experience at the center when the engineering scale gets this large?

posts.topic: G42's Post ... We're proud to see Inception, partner with the Department of Government Enablement to advance Abu Dhabi's vision of becoming the
posts.ready_comment: Sovereign AI infrastructure is becoming the real differentiator for government digital transformation, and Abu Dhabi is setting a standard the rest of the region will likely follow. Having led a $50M digital transformation across 15 hospitals under Saudi Vision 2030, I've seen firsthand how critical it is to build on trusted, locally governed AI foundations rather than patching in external tools after the fact. The alignment between Inception and the Department of Government Enablement signals something deeper than a typical partnership, it's a deliberate architecture decision for long-term data sovereignty. Curious how Inception is approaching the balance between regional customization and keeping pace with global model advancements?
